I am, however, heading up to Vancouver on Thursday for #AtmosphereConf. Looking forward to learning a ton and meeting new people. If you see me please say hi!

The news is out. I was selected as the next KubeCon co-chair 🙌 I will be helping with the content for #KubeConNA26 #KubeConEU27 and #KubeConNA27 🙂

Thank you @cncf.io team for the trust and honor 🙌 and many many folks 🙂

Looking forward to this new adventure and let's keep Cloud Native Moving 💪🤟

😭😭😭🙏 I got so many compliments about the talk. And the most important validation came from the most active maintainer on the project.

6️⃣ Kubernetes Agent Sandbox: Securing the agentic wave with gVisor isolation.

2️⃣ AI-Conformant: GKE is now officially certified for ecosystem interoperability. 
3️⃣ MCP Server: The new standard for connecting AI models to your clusters. 
4️⃣ llm-d: The new CNCF-native standard for distributed inference.

recent_posts.text: Ramesh Avutu shows the architecture end to end with some tips around cold starts, DLQ monitoring, and when to use ScaledJobs over long running workers. Check it out if you're running queue consumers on Kubernetes and want to stop paying for idle compute. (4/4)

You can scale to zero when there is no work and scale up quickly when messages arrive. The article walks through a full setup on the Elastic Kubernetes Service (#EKS) with Pod Identity and no static credentials needed. (3/4)

https://lckhd.eu/JdOpjg

Running async queue workers on #Kubernetes with the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (#HPA) often means pods sitting idle because CPU never spikes enough to trigger a scale down. 🧵
